course_description:
Statistical inference; parametric models; point estimation; properties of estimators; maximum likelihood (ML) and properties of ML estimators; confidence intervals; hypothesis testing; goodness-of-fit tests; Bayesian inference; ANOVA; linear and logistic regression. The is the Advanced Science version of STAT2004.

STAT2904 provides a foundation of mathematical principles behind statistical inference, with an emphasis on understanding statistical models and being able to apply statistical methods to data. This involves the mathematical derivation of results. The course will cover a range of statistical procedures and the concepts underlying the construction and analysis of models for various types of data.

Additional timetable information
No classes on the following public holidays:
14 August (Ekka)
7 October (King's birthday)
If your practical occurs on a Public Holiday, then please arrange to attend another practical that week. Alternatively, one practical each week will be recorded and you can view the recording on Blackboard if desired.
Practicals start in Week 2.
All classes will be conducted on campus. Consult your personal timetable for times and locations. Students are expected to attend these sessions in person unless they have a valid reason for being unable to attend (such as illness).
Important: if you are ill, then do not attend any classes in person. Alternative arrangements can be organised – consult Blackboard for details.
course_aims: The aim of STAT2904 is to provide a foundation of mathematical principles behind statistical inference, with an emphasis on understanding statistical models and being able to apply statistical methods to real data. The course will cover a range of statistical procedures and the concepts underlying the construction and analysis of models for various types of data.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain the mathematical principles behind the statistical procedures
Interpret statistical results and draw conclusions, presenting these in a report.
Construct and analyse models to use for various types of data

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit each scanned or typed solutions online as a single PDF via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
To allow quick feedback to all students (and solutions), maximum extension for assignment is 1 week. See ADDITIONAL ASSESSMENT INFORMATION for extension and deferred examination information relating to this assessment item.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item then the listed penalty will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point any submission will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is recorded from the time the submission is due.
